title: Mass General Brigham, nurses called to talk at State House amid biggest nursing strike in Mass.
summary: Approximately 4,000 nurses at Brigham and Women's Hospital walked off the job for a one-day strike—the largest ever in Massachusetts—in protest over pay raises and insurance costs. Governor Maura Healey summoned Mass General Brigham and the Massachusetts Nurses Association to the State House on July 8 for a mediated contract negotiation, with Boston Mayor Michelle Wu also helping arrange the talks. The strike marked the first time Brigham and Women's Hospital had experienced a nursing walkout since the hospital was integrated into the Mass General Brigham health system. Nurses staged a boisterous protest at the hospital's Francis Street location beginning at 7 a.m., waving signs and drawing support from passing motorists. The article body beyond this lede is behind a STAT+ paywall, limiting deeper factual details about contract terms or negotiation specifics.
analysis_notes: Article largely behind STAT+ paywall; only the headline, subheadline, and opening paragraph are accessible. Key facts (4,000 nurses, one-day strike, first at Brigham, Healey mediation) come from the freely available lede. No biotech-specific data—drugs, trials, or deals mentioned. Low priority for biotech operators; may be relevant to healthcare infrastructure investors tracking labor dynamics at Mass General Brigham's hospital network.

# Mass General Brigham, nurses called to talk at State House amid biggest nursing strike in Mass.

## About 4,000 leave job for one day in fight over pay raises, insurance costs

- [Manage alerts for this article](https://www.statnews.com/my-account/edit/#emails)
- [Email this article](mailto:?subject=Brigham%20and%20Women%E2%80%99s%20Hospital%20nurses%20strike%20over%20pay%20raises,%20insurance%20costs&body=Brigham%20and%20Women%E2%80%99s%20Hospital%20nurses%20strike%20over%20pay%20raises,%20insurance%20costs%0A%0AAbout%204,000%20Brigham%20nurses%20walked%20off%20the%20job%20for%20one%20day%20as%20they%20fight%20with%20Mass%20General%20Brigham%20over%20pay%20increases%20and%20insurance%20costs.%0A%0Ahttps://www.statnews.com/2026/07/08/brigham-and-womens-hospital-boston-nurses-strike-largest-massachusetts-history/)
- [Share this article](https://www.statnews.com/2026/07/08/brigham-and-womens-hospital-boston-nurses-strike-largest-massachusetts-history/#)

![](https://www.statnews.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/s3___bgmp-arc_arc-feeds_generic-photos_to-arc_Kanto_-BrighamStrikeDay1_0016-645x645.jpg)Nurses strike outside Brigham and Women’s Hospital in Boston on Wednesday.Christian Kantosky for The Boston Globe

By Marin Wolf — Boston Globe and Jonathan Saltzman — Boston Globe

July 8, 2026

BOSTON — Governor Maura Healey has summoned the state’s largest health system and its [striking nurses](https://www.bostonglobe.com/2026/07/07/business/brigham-hospital-nurses-strike/?p1=Article_Inline_Text_Link) to the State House on Wednesday in an attempt to broker a new contract, according to the Massachusetts Nurses Association.

The calling of the late-afternoon meeting came hours after a boisterous start to Massachusetts’ biggest-ever nurses strike, and the first at Brigham and Women’s Hospital. Mayor Michelle Wu also helped arrange the meeting, the union said.

Advertisement

Thousands of Brigham nurses and supporters poured onto Francis Street near the hospital starting at 7 a.m., shaking cowbells, banging on plastic buckets and cheering at a deafening chorus of supportive honks from passing cars. The nurses, sporting “Union Strong” and “Brigham Nurses United” shirts, waved signs calling out management. “Value Nurses Like You Value Your Bonu$e$,” one sign read.

title: White House reviewing top contenders to lead FDA
summary: The White House is conducting final review and making a decision on the finalists for FDA Commissioner, according to person(s) familiar with the selection process. The three named finalists are Heidi Overton, identified as a White House adviser; Jeffrey Vacirca, an oncologist and health system executive; and Stephen Ferrara, a Department of Defense health affairs official. The full article is behind a STAT+ paywall, but this represents a significant leadership succession event for the agency that oversees drug approvals, medical device clearances, biologics regulation, and the pharmaceutical industry's most critical regulatory gateway. The selection will shape FDA priorities in areas including drug pricing policy, generic drug supply chain resilience, AI-driven drug review capacity, and post-pandemic public health posture during the remainder of the Trump administration.
analysis_notes: Full article text is behind STAT+ paywall; summary based on headline, lead paragraph, and contextual bio of author Daniel Payne who covers health industry-government intersection.

[Subscribe](https://www.statnews.com/stat-plus/) or [log in](https://www.statnews.com/login/) to STAT+ to unlock email alerts

WASHINGTON — The top contenders to lead the Food and Drug Administration have been sent to the White House for a final review and decision, according to a person familiar with the process.

The finalists include Heidi Overton, a White House adviser; Jeffrey Vacirca, an oncologist and health system executive; and Stephen Ferrara, a health affairs official at the Department of Defense.

title: RFK Jr. plans to create list of injuries caused by Covid-19 vaccines
summary: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is preparing to initiate the process of compiling a prespecified list of injuries presumed to be caused by Covid-19 vaccines under the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program (VICP). Individuals whose conditions appear on the list could more readily seek and receive government compensation without having to prove causation case-by-case. The specific conditions that may make the list remain unspecified, drawing close scrutiny from outside experts and medical observers. Kennedy has long been publicly critical of vaccine safety, stating none have been adequately tested, and he plans to overhaul the separate federal vaccine injury compensation program. This signal could reshape legal liability exposure for Covid-19 vaccine manufacturers and influence future vaccine uptake among the public.
analysis_notes: Article content is behind a STAT+ paywall; analysis based on headline, lead paragraph, and metadata only. The reginfo.gov link (RIN 0906-AB31) references the actual Notice of Proposed Rulemaking in the federal register process, but the full text was not accessible for deeper due diligence.

# RFK Jr. plans to create list of injuries caused by Covid-19 vaccines

## List would make it easier for people to get compensated for vaccine injuries

[Subscribe](https://www.statnews.com/stat-plus/) or [log in](https://www.statnews.com/login/) to STAT+ to unlock email alerts

WASHINGTON — Health secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is preparing to make it easier for people to claim that they were injured by a Covid-19 vaccine and receive compensation.

Kennedy is set to [start the process of compiling a list](https://www.reginfo.gov/public/do/eAgendaViewRule?pubId=202510&RIN=0906-AB31) of injuries that are presumed to be caused by Covid shots. People with those conditions could then ask for compensation from the government. It’s not clear what conditions may make the list — and that’s something that outside experts are keeping a close eye on.

Advertisement

Kennedy has long been critical of vaccines, saying none have been adequately safety tested. He’s said that he plans to overhaul a similar but separate program that provides compensation for individuals who claim injury by a vaccine recommended by the federal government.
